prose:
Method provenance (follows chemkit Calculation-Reporting Standards)

    • Property calculated: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for pyridine; identification of the most electrophilic (max f⁺) and most nucleophilic (max f⁻) atomic sites.  
    • Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting), as implemented in PySCF (through chemkit v engine).  
    • Geometry provenance: single-point analysis on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in  
      /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz  
      (no additional optimization; reported as DFT//input-XYZ).  
    • Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  
    • Total molecular charge = 0, spin multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation (N−1) and anion (N+1) states treated as doublets (M = 2).  
    • Convergence/caveats: all three SCF calculations (N, N−1, N+1) converged; integrity.status = “ok”, trustworthy = true.  
    • Warnings returned by engine (verbatim and none omitted):  
        – "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"  
        – "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"  
        – "common header present"  
      (All checks marked ok; no additional warnings were reported.)  
    • Live run log: the SCF/analysis output was streamed in real time to  
      /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpw7jw2vio/fukui_20260706-191516.out  (you can `tail -f` this file for full details).  
    • Command that generated the result (from result JSON `cli_invocation`):  
      `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpw7jw2vio/live.json`

    f⁻  = [0.064122671, 0.0061877146, 0.11823139, 0.11826260, 0.11206595, 0.11200985, 0.08284851, 0.098179487, 0.09818226, 0.094960093, 0.094949479]
    f⁰  = [0.27417419, 0.16532999, 0.12927616, 0.12932924, 0.20584611, 0.20574345, −0.029332343, −0.017752308, −0.017738395, −0.022428103, −0.022447986]
    Dual descriptor = [0.42010305, 0.31828454, 0.022089544, 0.022133277, 0.18756031, 0.18746721, −0.22436171, −0.23186359, −0.23184131, −0.23477639, −0.23479493]

Reactive sites (Mulliken-based):
    • Most electrophilic (highest f⁺): Atom 1 (N) with f⁺ = 0.48422572.
    • Most nucleophilic (highest f⁻): Atom 4 (C) with f⁻ = 0.11826260.

The headline value reported above (0.48422572) is the maximum f⁺ value, identifying the nitrogen atom (index 0) as the site most susceptible to nucleophilic attack under this level of theory.
